title: Glassy-Jelly Light Venezuelan Pul Biber Nigiri
description: A mediterranean-inspired dish featuring the sweet and spicy flavors of pul biber, served in a glassy-jelly light venezuelan style, shaped into nigiri form.
instructions: Combine 1 cup of sugar and 1 cup of water in a saucepan and heat the mixture until the sugar dissolves.
Add 2 tablespoons of agar agar to the sugar mixture and stir to combine.
Bring the sugar mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat and let it simmer for 5 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in 2 teaspoons of pul biber, 1 tablespoon of lemon juice, 2 tablespoons of honey, and 1/4 teaspoon of salt.
Let the mixture cool slightly, then stir in 2 tablespoons of rice vinegar.
Spoon the mixture into a glass jar or container and let it chill in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.
Prepare the nigiri by cutting a small piece of cucumber into a nigiri shape and placing a small amount of the sugar mixture onto the cucumber.
Drizzle a small amount of sesame oil onto the sugar mixture and sprinkle with sesame seeds.
Garnish with a small piece of scallion and serve immediately.
